title: chmod 400 (r--------) — Linux File Permissions Explained
description: What does chmod 400 mean? Owner read-only, no one else. Interactive permission breakdown, copy-paste commands, security analysis, and common mistakes explained.

title: chmod 444 (r--r--r--) — Linux File Permissions Explained
description: What does chmod 444 mean? Read-only for everyone. Interactive permission breakdown, copy-paste commands, security analysis, and common mistakes explained.

title: chmod 500 (r-x------) — Linux File Permissions Explained
description: What does chmod 500 mean? Owner read & execute only, no one else. Interactive permission breakdown, copy-paste commands, security analysis, and common mistakes explained.

title: chmod 555 (r-xr-xr-x) — Linux File Permissions Explained
description: What does chmod 555 mean? Read & execute for everyone, no writing. Interactive permission breakdown, copy-paste commands, security analysis, and common mistakes explained.

title: chmod 600 (rw-------) — Linux File Permissions Explained
description: What does chmod 600 mean? Owner read/write only — private file. Interactive permission breakdown, copy-paste commands, security analysis, and common mistakes explained.

title: chmod 640 (rw-r-----) — Linux File Permissions Explained
description: What does chmod 640 mean? Owner read/write, group read, others nothing. Interactive permission breakdown, copy-paste commands, security analysis, and common mistakes explained.

description: What does chmod 644 mean? Owner read/write, others read-only. Interactive permission breakdown, copy-paste commands, security analysis, and common mistakes explained.

description: What does chmod 664 mean? Owner & group read/write, others read. Interactive permission breakdown, copy-paste commands, security analysis, and common mistakes explained.

title: chmod 666 (rw-rw-rw-) — Linux File Permissions Explained
description: What does chmod 666 mean? Read/write for everyone, no execute. Interactive permission breakdown, copy-paste commands, security analysis, and common mistakes explained.

title: chmod 700 (rwx------) — Linux File Permissions Explained
description: What does chmod 700 mean? Owner only — full access, no one else. Interactive permission breakdown, copy-paste commands, security analysis, and common mistakes explained.

description: What does chmod 750 mean? Owner full, group read & execute, others nothing. Interactive permission breakdown, copy-paste commands, security analysis, and common mistakes explained.

description: What does chmod 755 mean? Owner full, others read & execute. Interactive permission breakdown, copy-paste commands, security analysis, and common mistakes explained.

description: What does chmod 770 mean? Owner & group full access, others nothing. Interactive permission breakdown, copy-paste commands, security analysis, and common mistakes explained.

description: What does chmod 775 mean? Owner & group full, others read & execute. Interactive permission breakdown, copy-paste commands, security analysis, and common mistakes explained.

description: What does chmod 777 mean? Full access for everyone. Interactive permission breakdown, copy-paste commands, security analysis, and common mistakes explained.
